body_txt: Famous Last Words are defining last words of a character to the point that they represent the very climax of that character's development , leaving a lasting impression on other characters and fans. This is difficult to write despite normally being just a few words. Any misuse and it can come off as cheesy . Done perfectly and it can even garner the respect of fans that hated a certain character . An affectionate subversion is that characters utter what they expect will be their last words but survive after all. Compare Sedgwick Speech where a character gets killed after their speech or one-liner, usually in an ironic fashion . See also "Facing the Bullets" One-Liner (where a character says something before knowingly facing death), Pre-Mortem Catchphrase (when a Catchphrase ends up being their last words) and Dying Curse (when the words are a curse). See also Dying Declaration of Love and Dying Declaration of Hate . Often a Tear Jerker . Contrast Lame Last Words which are innocuous or pathetic — after all, not everybody has the right to famous last words. However, such last words can actually become famous if memetic enough. Also compare and contrast Ironic Last Words where a character says something that turns out to be ironic in a certain fashion. It should be noted that this doesn't exclusively refer to the saying for when someone makes a statement that's soon (fatally) proven to be wrong . A No Recent Examples rule applies to Real Life examples of this trope . Examples shouldn't be added until 50 years after the death. Massive Spoilers Within , so don't go further if you don't want them.
